rulings.Case Title: CONSTANCE BOURGES VS. DANY SCHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment Defendant'S 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgment On Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 8, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF CONSTANCE BOURGES' Motion To Vacate Judgment Defendant'S 4th Demurrer With Leave To Amend Plaintiff'S 5th Complaint, Vacate Judgment On Sanctions: Plaintiff's peremptory challenge is denied: (1) it is not supported by a declaration and (2) due to the nature of the motion. Hearing Required regarding Motion to Vacate. Defendant to present a proof of service for Demurrer to the Fourth Amended Complaint. (501/REQ) - LATE TENTATIVE

rulings.Case Title: RESNICK, DOUGLAS MARK, AS TRUSTEE OF THE PEARL VS. ANGELA CRANDALL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena And For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 8, 2014, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT GREGORY RESNICK's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena And For Protective Order GRANTED. Escrow file contains protected financial information, which is not relevant to Gregory Resnick's mental capacity at the relevant times and any information regarding alleged fraud on the Trust may be obtained by other less intrusive means. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 363 VALENCIA STREET LLC VS. HANIEL OLIVERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Request To Resolve The Procedural Issue Related To The Court'S Jurisdiction To Hear Motion To Enforce Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 & To Reinstate The Court'S Ruling On The Hearing Held On 08/13/2014
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 8, 2014, Line 9: PLAINTIFF 363 VALENCIA STREET LLC's Motion To Resolve The Procedural Issue Related To The Court'S Jurisdiction To Hear Motion To Enforce Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6 and To Reinstate The Court'S Ruling On The Hearing Held On 08/13/2014: GRANTED in part as follows. Parties Stipulation for Entry of Judgment is enforceable upon setting aside of dismissal. Parties, represented by counsel, stipulated that a judgment may be entered in this case upon breach. The mechanism for entry of judgment and the breaches are detailed. The terms of the stipulation were to be enforceable for 12 months notwithstanding a dismissal. Dismissal was to be filed within 2 days of execution of the stipulation. Further, renewed motion for entry of judgment is properly brought in light of Court's order of May 7, 2014 denying the motion solely based on (1) lack of admissible evidence submitted with the moving papers and (2) Court's ruling not to consider evidence submitted with reply as opposing party did not have sufficient opportunity to respond to it. Farber v. Bay View Terrace (2006) 141 Cal.App.4th 1007. (501/REQ)
